item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great, I really wish this had gotten more attention.

1+

item.title: Re: Author Post - The Disintegration Loops
item.content:encoded: Audio of 4842-Recording Epsilon was provided by user carolynn ivy. Train sounds in audio provided by user tim.kahn of Freesounds. Huge thanks to carolynn for doing all one hour and one minute of the audio recording.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a shame this is as low-rated as it is. It captures the essence of the album it's named after perfectly — a slow, impending deterioration, corroding with each repetitive turn of the tape. — with the same haunting atmosphere to boot. I had to reread this a couple of times to piece everything together, but I think the subtlety works to the article's benefit. Assuming I have the correct interpretation, then any more detail would ruin the message this is trying to send. How some decays are much better hidden than others.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The transcripts are a tad too many to go read through and it just feels unnecessarily too much. Either you could combine some of them into two or three big transcripts or omit a few of them to make it easier to parse through.

This is just a personal thing but I'd appreciate to see what kind of boombox it is (what brand, type, etc.) or if it doesn't have any, you could just state that it has no branding on itself because it looks a little… "amateurish"? I'm not sure if that's the right word but I think that the physical description of the object could be improved instead of just stating that it's just a boombox.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In some of the addenda you used the speakers first initial to represent their name. This may come off as an attempt to get out of doing more typing

This is a stylistic choice, and one which I've used before, as well as other authors.

fix up some grammar

I can't possibly know what grammar to fix if I'm not told or given examples of bad/incorrect grammar. I understand if you don't wish to get deep into it, but even pointing out one example would go a long way in me actually fixing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was pretty good, except for the logs. In some of the addenda you used the speakers first initial to represent their name. This may come off as an attempt to get out of doing more typing, Tip: just do the typing, fix up some grammar, and your good. Also explain the whole train thing and what the whole spiel is a bit more.

Hard Upvote but an upvote.

item.title: Re: Here's my linebyline of the description
item.content:encoded: I think the redactions are fine. I'll fix up everything else though.

item.title: Here's my linebyline of the description
item.content:encoded:
So, I can see that a ton of effort went into this piece, but I'm personally not feeling it. However, aside from just generally not liking the logs, the description strikes me as odd, so per request of the author, here is my line by line of the description.

Line by line
Close

SCP-4842-1 is a boombox which plays at maximum volume without the need for a power source.

Off the bat, this sentence feels like it lacks clinical tone. It's not what I would call "aclinical", but the wordings could be tweaked a bit to make it feel MORE clinical, like, not using words like "plays", or the somewhat colloquial use of "maximum volume" (is it important that we know how loud it is? How do we know it's at maximum volume? this inclusion brings up more questions than answers, and doesn't add to the piece overall.

The only sounds that have played since its discovery and subsequent containment are field recordings of trains passing nearby the microphone.

The clinical tone feels off on this one too, because of the phrases "the only sounds" and "nearby the microphone", which have the same 'not quite clinical' vibe to the previous sentence.

SCP-4842-2 is a boombox which manifests at 40°██'██.█"N 74°██'██.█"W once every 177 days, thereafter playing at maximum volume without the need for a power source.

Aside from the obvious disparities, this feels like it could be lumped in with the first section, like "SCP-4842 refers to two boomboxes…" and then describe each one. Also, I know including numbers would be useless, but visually, those redactions are really distracting. Ultimately, including the coordinates and then removing them adds nothing, and feels like something you should just omit.

only two individuals are present on all recordings, identified as Jensen Kortland and Alix Cuthbertson.

Remove "only".

Interviews with both show that neither were aware of nor recognize SCP-4842's associated phenomena, and that neither have visited █████████████, ████████, having lived on Martha's Vineyard together since April 7th, 2013.

A few issues here. "Interviews with both show that neither" is awkwardly phrased. I would side in favor of removing "both" and saying something like "Interviews with the individuals in question" or something more clinical.

"Recognize" seems like a strange word to use when describing phenomena. I would reword this along the lines of "Interviews with the individuals in question have revealed that neither are aware of SCP-4842 or its associated phenomena".

Another example of weird redactions, this time, referencing something that we haven't seen before and therefore, doesn't even work as a callback redaction since, again, it's a brand new redaction. This unknown location might as well be omitted.

item.title: Author Post - The Disintegration Loops
item.content:encoded:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Disintegration_Loops

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mjnAE5go9dI

`
– hide block

haha yeah "severed ears" same when i listen to william basinski am i RIGHT music bros
